new_classes.definition_en: A spirituous beverage of heading 2208 produced by distillation or rectification of fermented grain or potato mash (or other agricultural raw materials), characterised by an organoleptic character with no predominant aroma, flavour or colour derived from the raw materials used, as described in HS Explanatory Note to heading 2208, paragraph 3, point 5.

new_classes.label_de: Wodka in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von höchstens 2 l (KN 2208 60 11)
new_classes.definition_en: Vodka of CN heading 2208 60, presented in containers of a capacity not exceeding 2 litres, as classified under CN code 2208 60 11.

coverage_explanation: The legal note for CN 2208 60 11 identifies the product as vodka (a spirit of heading 2208) in containers not exceeding 2 litres. The key distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) product type = vodka (subclass of SpiritousBeverage2208), and (2) container size ≤ 2 L (via BFO parthood restriction to SmallContainer). The note cross-references HS Explanatory Note to heading 2208, paragraph 3, point 5, which specifies the production method (distillation/rectification of fermented grain, potato or other agricultural raw materials) and the characteristic absence of a predominant aroma/flavour/colour. These production process and organoleptic quality criteria are acknowledged in the Vodka class definition but are not fully formalised as OWL restrictions (e.g. no explicit distillation process restriction or OrganolepticCharacteristicsQuality restriction is added here due to the risk of over-committing without a dedicated vodka distillation process class). Coverage is therefore estimated at 0.7.
